title: Endpoint Catalog Manifest (v1)
description:
Signed pointer to current catalog version. Pull endpoint MUST serve with Cache-Control: no-store. Sidecar polls at least every 5 minutes (Sidecar §8 critical_revocation_max_stale).

Spec references:
  - Sidecar Architecture spec v1alpha1 §8 (endpoint discovery + signed catalog)
  - Stage 2 POC Topology spec v1alpha1 §8.2.4 (Endpoint Catalog atomic update model), §10.4 (POC Tier 3)

Compatibility: additive-only schema evolution; new fields tolerated at the consumer.

current_catalog_version_id.description: Identifier of the immutable catalog object pointed to. Format: ctlg-<rfc3339-utc>-<discriminator>, where <discriminator> is either rev<int> or a UUIDv7 in 'simple' (32 hex chars) form. UUIDv7 is preferred for collision-free monotonic uniqueness.

current_catalog_url.description: HTTPS URL to fetch the immutable catalog object. CDN-cacheable for 24h.

current_catalog_hash.description: sha256 hash of the catalog object body (hex lowercase, 64 chars)

valid_until.description: Manifest expiry. Sidecar fail-closes for enforcement routes if no valid manifest within last_verified_critical_version_age > 5min (Sidecar §8).

signing_key_id.description: Key id used to sign the manifest; sidecar verifies against pinned trust root.

signature.description: Base64-encoded ed25519 signature over canonical JSON of the manifest body (excluding signature field).

tenant_overrides.description: Optional per-tenant catalog override pointers; absent means tenant uses default.
